calendar.matters:
Petition For Appointment Of Successor Trustee.
Off Calendar Nov-30-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert L. Hobson in propria persona.

The Court stated that the signature was not notarized. Mr. Hobson stated that at the time of the signing a notary was not available and that by his interpretation even though the signature is not notarized it still is a valid Trust. The Court inquired about the missing heir. Mr. Hobson stated that Linda McKenna was an employee at Bank of America, that at the time she had not been there long, that he had contacted employees of Bank of America that knew her and they thought she had moved to Los Angeles, that he has searched online in an attempt to find her, and that he specifically searched Los Angeles and New York with no luck. Mr. Hobson presented additional argument regarding the search for Ms. McKenna. The Court then granted the petition (an order for the same was signed in open court).

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Arlene Howell CSR#2542. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.

Petition For Appointment Of Successor Trustee.
Off Calendar Nov-16-2009 Continued To Nov-30-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: No appearance was made by any party, counsel or interested person.

The matter was continued to November 30, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.
